hero.title: Jashmal
hero.description: A living tool for the study of the Torah, Talmud, Midrash, Kabbalah and Chasidut. You choose a text; the analysis descends through the four worlds of PaRDeS.
hero.etymology: From the chashmal of Ezekiel 1:4 — chash (silence) and mal (speech).
hero.guided: I've never studied this. Take me by the hand
hero.guidedHint: Start with The Threshold — a first lesson that asks for nothing beforehand.

study.title: Study engine

study.deepHint: Brings the real connected sources from Sefaria (Targum, commentators, Midrash, Kabbalah, Chasidut). Deeper, ~1-2 min.
study.depthNote: The deep study gathers the real sources connected to this passage before analyzing it. It takes longer, but is more faithful.

about.title: About Jashmal
about.s1Body: Jashmal (חַשְׁמַל) is a word that appears in the vision of the prophet Ezekiel (Ezekiel 1:4): "and from the midst of the fire, like the gleam of the chashmal." It is one of the most enigmatic words of the Torah — the sages describe it as a radiance, an incandescent amber, an electrum glowing from within the fire. It is neither a mere color nor a mere metal: it is living light caught in matter.
about.s2Body: The Talmud (Chagigah 13b) reads the name as two joined words: "chash" (חַשׁ, silence) and "mal" (מַל, speech). The angels called chashmalim are sometimes silent and sometimes speak: silent when the divine Word emanates, speaking when they transmit it. That is why Jashmal is "the voice of silence": first the text is heard in silence, and only then does one speak. That is the method of this place.
about.s3Body: Jashmal is a living tool of study. You choose a text from the Torah, Talmud, Midrash, Kabbalah or Chasidut, and the analysis descends through the four worlds of PaRDeS: Pshat (literal), Remez (allusion), Drash (interpretation) and Sod (secret). The engine gathers the real sources connected to each passage — the Targumim, the commentators, the Zohar, the Arizal — as a student would who opens many books at once.
about.s4Body: Knowledge is only complete when it descends into the world of action. Contemplation alone is not enough: the light of wisdom (Chochmah) must descend to Malchut, to daily life, to an act of kindness. Jashmal seeks that each study bring you closer to God, to yourself and to your neighbor — that silence learn to speak, and that the word become deed.

chat.hint: Have a question about a word or concept? Ask here.

audio.hint: Narrated study, ready to listen in the car 🚗

tree.title: Tree of Life
tree.subtitle: The 10 Sefirot across the three pillars, joined by 22 paths of light. Touch a Sefira: it lights up and reveals its light.
tree.refHint: Click the rays to study the textual reference
tree.scrollHint: ↕ Scroll to zoom · Click a Sefira
tree.fractalNote: Each Sefira contains all 10 Sefirot. Keter of Atzilut contains 10; Chochmah contains 10... The structure is fractal and infinite.
tree.emptyPanel: Touch any of the ten spheres to let its light be revealed: its name, its pillar, and its function in creation.

step1.title: Welcome to Jashmal
step1.desc: This is your study room for Torah, Talmud and Kabbalah. Jashmal analyzes each passage using the PaRDeS method — in depth, in your language. Let me show you how it works.

step2.title: Search any text
step2.desc: Type a reference directly: Genesis 1:1, Shabbat 2a, Zohar Bereshit. Or use the categories below to browse book by book.

step3.title: Browse by category
step3.desc: Choose from Torah, Talmud, Midrash, Kabbalah or Chassidut. Then select the book, chapter and verse you want to study.

step4.title: Analysis appears here
step4.desc: Once you choose a text and press Study, Jashmal generates a structured analysis: translation, classic commentators, PaRDeS and Chassidic synthesis.

step5.title: Your personal tutor
step5.desc: Have a question about a word or concept? Tap this button to open the tutor. Jashmal answers your questions in real time, without losing your place in the study.

step6.title: Change the language
step6.desc: The entire analysis is generated in the chosen language. In Farsi, the interface switches to right-to-left writing.

step7.title: Begin your study
step7.desc: That's it. Type a reference or pick a category and press Study. Whenever you want to see this tour again, tap the ? button in the corner.

letra.name: Name
letra.mapHint: The letter as a multidimensional node.
letra.connectionsHint: Luminous paths into the study Universe.

misterios.title: Mysteries
misterios.intro: Studies that open the hidden layers of the text — gematria, letter permutation, and secrets of the Zohar. Each one is a doorway into the study engine.
misterios.thread: The thread that binds them: «Two Edges» (שְׁנֵי פִּיּוֹת) — the same act, depending on the angle, can be the fall or the redemption.
misterios.availableInFull: This study isn't in your language yet. You can read it in these languages.
misterios.notAvailable: English version coming soon — read this study in:

gematria.heading: Gematria
gematria.intro: Each Hebrew letter is a number. These numbers return again and again — divine Names, sefirot, structures of creation. Tap each number to open its secret.
